topic_title: The Judgment Framework — Q3: Who is accountable if this fails?

positional_bias.note: Positions 1 and 3 each hold 2/6 = 33.3%, well under the 40% cap. No shuffle needed.

length_bias.auto_correction_applied: Extended one distractor per failing question to exceed correct-answer length. Correct-answer text unchanged. Distractor extensions remain plausible misconceptions.

notes: q1: liability, blame, developer-only — all documented accountability misconceptions.
q2: profits=responsibility (developer), employees=bank (operator confusion), harmed=accountable (end-user confusion) — all plausible.
q3: timing flexibility, legal protection purpose, regulated-industry standard — all plausible under-pressure rationalizations.
q4: currently-identifiable-is-enough, verify-yourself-required, temporary-employee-exclusion — all plausible three-criteria confusions.
q5: document=transfer, framework-only-when-easy, self-nominate — all plausible under time pressure.
q6: developer-responsible (design confusion), human-in-loop-required (automation misconception), partial-correct/corporate-liability — all capture the §8 misconceptions directly.

coverage_map.lo: LO1 — define accountability vs liability vs blame
LO4 — three criteria: authority, knowledge, nameability
LO5 — identify accountable person; escalate when none exists
LO6 — recognise AI-responsible, clicked-submit, automated misconceptions

notes: All stems use plain language. Q3 used contextually with meaning established by the stem.
No question requires knowledge beyond 9.9's corpus.
All distractors are beginner misconceptions about Q3 concepts — none require advanced/adjacent knowledge.

auto_correction_applied.method: Extended one distractor per question to exceed correct-answer length; correct-answer text unchanged; distractor plausibility verified.

verdict: PASS — all five checks pass on cycle 2. Cycle 1 failed length-bias: 4/6 questions (q2, q4, q5, q6) had correct-answer text longer than mean by >20%. Auto-corrected by extending one distractor per question past the correct-answer length — correct text unchanged, extended distractors remain plausible. Positional distribution balanced (33% max share). Full LO coverage 1:1. All distractors capture documented misconceptions.
